rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Nick Volente, Kamran Ardebilchi And Jahangir Ardebilchi'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Site Inspection,And Deposition From Plaintiff Paul Gerrard And Request For Tertminating Sanctions (Continued From 5/11/12)
rulings.Rulings: set for hearing on friday, May 18, 2012, Line 1. Defendants Nick Volente, Kamran Ardebilchi And Jahangir Ardebilchi'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Site Inspection,And Deposition From Plaintiff Paul Gerrard And Request For Tertminating Sanctions (Continued From 5/11/12). Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted as unopposed. Moving party should provide an appropriate order to the court for signing at 9:00 am on May 18, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EVELYN POQUEZ VS. SUNCOR HOLDINGS-COPII, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 18, 2012, Line 5, DEFENDANT SUNCOR HOLDINGS-COPII, LLC, SUNCOR HOLDINGS, LLC, GSM PARTNERS, LLC and PACIFIC TBG, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first and second causes of action and granted as to the third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, there is a triable issue whether defendants acted with due diligence and made good faith best efforts to obtain the permits and entitlements in a reasonably prompt manner. Defendants fails to demonstrate that plaintiff cannot establish causation and defendants' own architect indicated that the permits could be obtained in 6 to 8 months. The contract does not limit plaintiff's right to recover monetary damages. As to the second cause of action, there is a triable issue whether defendants repudiated plaintiff's option rights by selling the property prior to giving plaintiff a reasonable time to exercise those rights. The third cause of action adds nothing substantive legally or factually beyond the first two claims and is thus superfluous. As to the fourth cause of action, defendants sustained their burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether Suncor was a franchisor or an affiliate of a franchisor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALI KIA SHABAHANGI et al VS. NADER SHABAHANGI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 18, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTS NADER SHABAHANGI and AGESONG, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. All claims appear to fall within the scope of the broad language of the arbitration provision of the operating agreement. However, the arbitrator is given the authority to determine that certain claims are not subject to arbitration and any such claims will be stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. The claims pertaining to the Operating Agreement against non-signatory AgeSong Inc. are arbitrable based on the alter ego allegations. Plaintiffs have not demonstrated any prejudice in defendants' delay in moving to compel arbitration. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes with case number 508919 as the lead case and all further filings in that case only. Both actions are stayed until the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE BECERRA VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Suspension Driving Priviledge
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 18, 2012, Line 14, Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Suspension Driving Priviledge. Granted. The weight of the evidence shows that Petitioner rebutted the three-hour presumption found in Vehicle Code section 23152(b). PAS tests are presumptively valid. (Manriquez v. Gourley (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 1227, 1232; Evidence Code section 664.) Respondent failed to produce evidence to show Petitioner's PAS results were invalid. Additionally, Respondent failed to produce evidence that shows Petitioner's BAC was over the legal limit while she was driving. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
